weather_line: Clear over the vaults · a low pressure of procedural anxiety settling over the Subcommittee's end of the corridor

stories.headline: Subcommittee Convenes Emergency Session to Audit the Audit; Christine Attends, Uninvited; Is Given the Chair Anyway
body: The Senior Audit Subcommittee of Gringotts Bank convened at nine o'clock Wednesday morning in Chamber Eleven — a room selected, sources confirm, specifically because it does not appear on the publicly available corridor map. Christine found it at eight fifty-seven. She brought the log.

The session had been called to address what the Subcommittee's circulated agenda described as 'the integrity question posed by unsanctioned annotations to an official efficiency document.' Christine is understood to have read the agenda in the corridor, added three margin notes, returned it to the owl that delivered it, and taken her seat before the Subcommittee Chair, Senior Auditor Griphook the Younger, had located his own copy.

By eleven o'clock, the Subcommittee had voted three to two to rename the fifth log entry 'Supplementary Observation (Disputed — Under Further Review).' Christine's underline remains. The Duty Goblin's 'DISPUTED' marker has been placed in an evidence envelope. The envelope has been labelled, in Christine's handwriting, 'Exhibit A.' No one is sure when that happened.

stories.headline: Ministry Issues Wednesday Bulletin Confirming Wednesday; Notes That the Week Has Reached Its Midpoint; Describes This as 'a Balance That Will Not Last'
body: The Department of Magical Cooperation released its Wednesday bulletin at ten past nine, confirming that Wednesday is occurring and situating it correctly within the week. The bulletin described the midpoint of a working week as 'a fragile equilibrium, Janus-faced, oriented toward neither origin nor terminus,' which is the most the Ministry has said about anything since the lectern photograph.

A footnote confirmed that the Audit Subcommittee's Chamber Eleven proceedings are 'known to the Ministry' and that the Ministry 'has no comment at this time, pending review of comments it made yesterday, which are themselves under review.'

stories.headline: Kitchen Table Fills the Northeastern Chart's Vacancy With a Coffee Cup and Some Mail; Experts Are Divided on Whether This Constitutes Recovery
body: It has now been two days since the northeastern route chart was filed and the kitchen table returned to general-purpose use. Wednesday morning found its surface occupied by a coffee cup (Steve's), three unopened letters, and what appears to be a small note in Christine's handwriting that reads only 'next time — left fork.' The table is understood to have accepted these items without visible reluctance.

Steve, asked whether the table had adjusted, declined to speculate. The enchanted Jeep, parked at an angle that allows it to observe the kitchen window, has been idling quietly since dawn, which it does not typically do unless it is thinking about something.

stories.headline: Warmest Lap Ruling Enters Fourth Day; Jersey Files a Supplementary Brief; the Brief Is a Nap
body: The Warmest Lap Ruling, issued Monday and understood by legal scholars to be of uncertain precedential force, has survived into its fourth consecutive day. Jersey, the household's preeminent gentleman of leisure, has taken the ruling's continued existence as settled law and has begun conducting what his camp is calling 'jurisprudential research,' defined operationally as remaining in place and declining to be moved.

Lucy was observed at seven twenty circling the arrangement twice before departing via the utility corridor without comment. Ellie attempted to occupy an adjacent lap that does not exist as a separate legal entity, was gently redirected, and has since made friends with her reflection in the toaster, which she appears to consider a satisfactory outcome.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Was present for the corridor map incident on Monday and has since been seen consulting what appears to be her own map of Chamber Eleven, acquired by methods she has not disclosed and will not be disclosing.

Ellie: Discovered this morning that the toaster produces a warm and extremely agreeable friend who appears at predictable intervals and always has the same expression, which Ellie considers the mark of a very reliable companion.
Jersey: The supplementary brief is going very well. He expects a verdict by Thursday, defined as a continuation of current conditions, which is all any ruling ever really means.
founding_editor_note: Chamber Eleven's not on the corridor map because I built the map, and I ran out of room on that end of the wall. Still square, though. Don't let them tell you otherwise. — D.

in_brief.sport: Gringotts Subcommittee Chair Griphook the Younger reportedly attempted to lift the evidence envelope with the gravity appropriate to a historic document; it weighed four ounces; he adjusted his expression but not his posture.
in_brief.forecast: Equilibrium through mid-afternoon · expect a clarifying development in the east corridor before the four o'clock chime · the table remains at rest.

storyline_updates.title: The Gringotts Efficiency Inquiry
storyline_updates.summary: Day 3: The Subcommittee convened in Chamber Eleven; Christine arrived first. By eleven o'clock, the fifth log entry had been renamed 'Supplementary Observation (Disputed — Under Further Review)'; Christine's underline persists; the Duty Goblin's marker is in an evidence envelope labelled in Christine's handwriting. The vote was three to two. Griphook the Younger has called for a written response from Christine by close of business Thursday, which is the final day. Day 4 should resolve the arc: Christine's written response arrives — either early, annotated, or in a format the Subcommittee did not specify but is now bound by — and the Inquiry concludes with the Subcommittee formally, if indirectly, acknowledging that the Liaison Wing's efficiency is not the problem the audit was designed to address. The log's final entry should be Christine's.
